_comment: Civic-terms glossary (w8-02). Pins the translation for hard civic terms BEFORE bulk drafting, so a term is consistent everywhere it appears instead of translated ad hoc per string. `source` cites where the City's own translation of the term comes from (MOIA Language Access materials, an agency Language Access Plan) when one exists; otherwise it records the judgment call. `note` records any nuance a translator/reviewer should know. Extend this file — never a per-string ad hoc choice — when a new civic term needs pinning for a new language.

rfp.source: es: NYC MOCS/PASSPort public-facing Spanish procurement materials use "Solicitud de Propuestas"; all other shipping languages: no official City translation found — chose the standard local government-procurement term and kept the English acronym parenthetical, matching the existing es convention of keeping the acronym visible on first use.

award.note: Used as both noun ("an award") and verb ("awarded to") in en; es collapses both onto "adjudicación"/"adjudicado". Every shipping-language dictionary must do the same collapse — do not introduce a second synonym for the verb form.

procurement.source: es: chosen by judgment call in the wave-6 hotfix (i18n.js SECTION_I18N.es); all other shipping languages: no official City translation found — mirrored the same judgment-call posture, using the standard civic-procurement term in each language. fr originally pinned "Marchés publics", but that renders as bare ASCII "March" once the accent on "Marchés" is stripped by the stray-English guard's [A-Za-z]+ word matcher — a literal collision with the curated month-name entry in test/standards/english_words.py (see that file's own "Latin-script addition" warning). Re-pinned to "Approvisionnement", which has no such collision and is an equally standard francophone government-procurement term.

rezoning.note: ULURP (Uniform Land Use Review Procedure) itself is never expanded/translated inline in any shipping language today — it stays an English acronym in body copy, same as PIN. This glossary entry covers the plain-language word "rezoning", not the ULURP acronym.

city_record.es: El Registro Municipal / The City Record
city_record.zh-Hans: 市政公报 / The City Record
city_record.note: The proper noun "The City Record" itself is never fully replaced — es keeps it as a citable link text (e.g. about.html) while using "Registro Municipal" for descriptive chrome (footer_lede, city_record_link). Every other shipping language follows the same split: descriptive chrome translates, the linked proper-noun citation keeps "The City Record" — except ur, which keeps the English institutional name in Latin script even for descriptive chrome (South Asian civic-register Urdu commonly keeps such names untransliterated) rather than translating it, a deliberate per-language variance from the split pattern, not an oversight.

pin.note: Procurement Identification Number. Kept as the untranslated acronym in every shipping language — es never expands or translates it, so none of the others do either.

community_board.source: no official City translation found for the non-es shipping languages — chose the standard civic-body term in each language (ur transliterates rather than translates, a common register choice for this specific civic term in South Asian Urdu), mirroring es's "Junta Comunitaria".

upset_price.note: DCAS auction term for the minimum acceptable bid. es already renders it as a plain-language "minimum price" rather than a literal translation of "upset"; every other shipping language follows the same plain-language approach (reserve/minimum price), not a literal rendering.

borough_names.en: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island
borough_names.es: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.zh-Hans: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.ru: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.bn: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.ht: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.ko: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.fr: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.pl: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.ar: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.ur: Brooklyn / Queens / Manhattan / Bronx / Staten Island (unchanged)
borough_names.note: Borough and neighborhood proper nouns (Brooklyn, Gowanus, 79 Rivington, …) stay in English/Latin script in es today (see i18n.js sugg_land_0/1/2) even though some LL30 languages' NYC press sometimes uses exonyms (e.g. Chinese-language press: 布鲁克林, 皇后区) or transliterations (e.g. Russian-language press: Бруклин, Куинс). Every shipping language follows the existing es precedent — proper nouns are not translated — for consistency with how the site already handles place names, not because exonyms/transliterations don't exist for any of these languages' press.

mandatory_inclusionary_housing.source: no official City translation found for the non-es shipping languages — mirrored es's pattern of a descriptive translation plus the (MIH) acronym parenthetical.
